records.ety: [OE. bileven (with pref. be- for AS. ge-), fr. AS. gelīfan, gelīfan; akin to D. gelooven, OHG. gilouban, G. glauben, OS. gilībian, Goth. galaubjan, and Goth. liubs dear. See Lief, a., Leave, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Believed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Believing.]

defs: To exercise belief in; to credit upon the authority or testimony of another; to be persuaded of the truth of, upon evidence furnished by reasons, arguments, and deductions of the mind, or by circumstances other than personal knowledge; to regard or accept as true; to place confidence in; to think; to consider; as, to believe a person, a statement, or a doctrine.
quotes: Our conqueror (whom I now Of force believe almighty).
King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets ?
Often followed by a dependent clause. I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.

defs: To have a firm persuasion, esp. of the truths of religion; to have a persuasion approaching to certainty; to exercise belief or faith.
quotes: Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
With the heart man believeth unto righteousness.
I will not believe so meanly of you.
